blocks:
Surah Ash-Shura

Meccan, and it consists of 53 verses.

Surah Ha Mim Ayn Sin Qaf, and it is called Surah Ash-Shura

It is Meccan. The number of its verses is fifty-three according to the Kufan count, and fifty in the remaining counts. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) حم. (1) Ḥā, Mīm. (٢) عسق. (2) ‘Ayn, Sīn, Qāf.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is the Wise (الحكيم), the Rewarder (المثيب), the All-Knowing (العالم), the All-Hearing (السميع), the All-Powerful (القادر), the Strong (القوي).

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is a letter from the disjointed (المقطوع) Greatest Name of Allah (اسم الله الأعظم). The Messenger and the Imam (peace be upon them both) compose it, and it becomes the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم) with which, if Allah is called upon, He responds. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Ayn Sin (عس) is the number of the years of al-Qa'im (peace be upon him). And Qaf (قاف) is a mountain surrounding the world, made of green emerald (زمردة خضراء). The greenness of the sky comes from that mountain. And the knowledge of everything is in عسق.

** كَذلِكَ يُوحِي إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ اللهُ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (3) Thus does Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise, reveal to you and to those who were before you. And it was recited (يُوحَى) with the opening (فَتْح) of the letter ḥāʾ (الحاء). ** لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ. (4) To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, and He is the All-exalted, the All-great. ** تَكادُ السَّماواتُ ** And it was recited with the yāʾ (بالياء) ** يَتَفَطَّرْنَ ** (splitting apart) They split apart (يَتَشَقَّقْنَ) due to the Greatness of Allah. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They crack (يَتَصَدَّعْنَ). And it was recited (يَنْفَطِرْنَ). ** مِنْ فَوْقِهِنَ ** (from above them) From their upper direction (جِهَتِهِنَّ الْفَوْقَانِيَّة) or from above the earths (الْأَرَضِينَ). ** وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** Al-Qummi said: [This forgiveness is] specifically for the believers among the repentant Shi'a (الشيعة التوابين), although the wording (لَفْظ) of the Ayah is general (عَامّ) while the meaning (الْمَعْنَى) is specific (خَاص). And in al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (and plead for forgiveness for those who are on the earth) meaning, from the believers (الْمُؤْمِنِينَ). ** أَلا إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ. (5) The heavens are almost splitting apart from above them, and the angels celebrate the praise of their Lord and plead for forgiveness for those who are on the earth. Look! Indeed Allah is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.

(٦) وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ اللهُ حَفِيظٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (6) But as for those who take guardians besides Him, Allah is watchful over them. He is a Watcher (رقيب) over their states (أحوالهم) and their deeds (أعمالهم), and He will requite them for them. ** وَما أَنْتَ ** O Muhammad, عَلَيْهِمْ بِوَكِيلٍ. and you are not a trustee over them. (٧) وَكَذلِكَ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ قُرْآناً عَرَبِيًّا لِتُنْذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرى ** (7) Thus have We revealed to you an Arabic Qur’ān, that you may warn the Mother of Cities the people of the Mother of Cities (أمّ القرى), which is Mecca (مكّة). The reason for its naming has already passed in Surat al-An'am. ** وَمَنْ حَوْلَها ** and those around it, (and) the rest of the earth (سائر الأرض). ** وَتُنْذِرَ يَوْمَ الْجَمْعِ ** and warn of the Day of Gathering, the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) when the creatures (الخلائق) will be gathered therein. ** لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ** there is no doubt in it. (This is) an interjection (اعتراض). ** فَرِيقٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَفَرِيقٌ فِي السَّعِيرِ.** A group will be in paradise and a group in the Blaze.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) addressed the people, then he raised his right hand, clenching his palm, and then said: "O people, do you know what is in my palm?" They said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "In it are the names of the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة), and the names of their fathers and their tribes until the Day of Resurrection." Then he raised his left hand and said: "O people, do you know what is in my palm?" They said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "The names of the people of the Fire (أهل النار), and the names of their fathers and their tribes until the Day of Resurrection." Then he said: "Allah has judged and been just; Allah has judged and been just." ** فَرِيقٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَفَرِيقٌ فِي السَّعِيرِ.** A group will be in paradise and a group in the Blaze.

(٨) وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَعَلَهُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (8) Had Allah wished, He would have made them a single nation. (as) guided. Al-Qummi (said): Had He wished to make all of them infallible (معصومين) like the angels, without natural dispositions (بلا طباع), He would have been able to do so. ** وَلكِنْ يُدْخِلُ مَنْ يَشاءُ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ** But He admits whomever He wishes into His mercy. by guidance (بالهداية). وَالظَّالِمُونَ ما لَهُمْ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ And the wrongdoers have no guardian or helper. meaning, He leaves them without a guardian (وليّ) or a helper (نصير) in His punishment.

(٩) أَمِ اتَّخَذُوا ** Rather, they have taken مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ فَاللهُ هُوَ الْوَلِيُّ وَهُوَ يُحْيِ الْمَوْتى وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (9) Have they taken guardians besides Him? But Allah is the [true] Guardian, and He revives the dead, and He has power over all things. (١٠) وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللهِ (10) Whatever thing you may differ about, its judgment rests with Allah. Al-Qummi (said regarding) وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ : concerning the schools of thought (المذاهب) and the religions (الأديان) you have chosen for yourselves, the judgment of all of that belongs to Allah on the Day of Resurrection. And it was said (regarding) وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ : concerning the interpretation (تأويل) of the ambiguous (متشابه), then refer back to the decisive (المحكم) part of the Book of Allah. ذلِكُمُ اللهُ رَبِّي عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ** (That is Allah, my Lord; in Him I put my trust): in the totality of affairs (في مجامع الأمور). وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ ** (and to Him I turn penitently): meaning, I return (أرجع).

(١١) فَاطِرُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَاجًا ** (11) (Originator of the heavens and the earth, He made for you mates from your own selves) Al-Qummi said: meaning, women. ** وَمِنَ الْأَنْعَامِ أَزْوَاجًا ** (and mates from the cattle) He said: meaning, male (ذكراً) and female (أنثى). ** يَذْرَؤُكُمْ فِيهِ ** (He multiplies you therein) He spreads you and multiplies you. Al-Qummi said: meaning, the offspring (النسل) that comes from males (الذكور) and females (الإناث). ** لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ ** (Nothing is like Him) Allah refuted those who described Allah (in anthropomorphic terms). It was said that the kāf (الكاف) is superfluous (زائدة). And it was said rather, the intent is exaggeration (المبالغة) in negating a likeness (المثل) from Him, for if it is negated from that which resembles Him and takes His place, then its negation from Him is more appropriate. In a sermon of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ (Nothing is like Him), since the thing (الشيء) is from His Will (مشيّة), and thus it does not resemble its Creator (مكوّنه)." This was narrated in Misbah al-Mutahajjid. ** وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ ** (and He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.) of everything that is heard and seen.

(١٢) لَهُ مَقالِيدُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (12) To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. Their treasures (خزائنهما). يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ** He expands the provision for whomever He wishes and tightens it. He expands and restricts according to His will. إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ Indeed He has knowledge of all things. So He does it as is appropriate. (١٣) شَرَعَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الدِّينِ ما وَصَّى بِهِ نُوحاً وَالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ وَما وَصَّيْنا بِهِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى وَعِيسى ** (13) He has prescribed for you the religion which He enjoined upon Noah, and what We have revealed to you, and what We enjoined upon Abraham, Moses, and Jesus: Meaning, He has prescribed for you the religion (شَرَعَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الدِّينِ)—the religion of Noah (peace be upon him) and Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his progeny) and those among them who were masters of the Divine Laws (أرباب الشرايع). And this is the fundamental principle (الأصل) and the common element (المشترك) among them. Al-Qummi (narrates), addressing the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny): أَنْ أَقِيمُوا الدِّينَ ** —to maintain the religion He said: Meaning, learn the religion (تعلّموا الدين), meaning Monotheism (التوحيد), establishing the prayer (اقام الصلاة), giving the Zakat (إيتاء الزكاة), fasting the month of Ramadan (صوم شهر رمضان), performing the Hajj of the House (حجّ البيت), the traditions (السّنن), the rulings (الأحكام) found in the Books, and acknowledging the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَلا تَتَفَرَّقُوا فِيهِ and do not be divided therein. And do not differ concerning it. كَبُرَ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** What you call them to is hard on the polytheists. It was great/grievous upon them. ما تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ ** He said: The mention of these Divine Laws (الشرائع). اللهُ يَجْتَبِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ Allah chooses for Himself whomever He wishes He selects and draws [them] to the religion. وَيَهْدِي إِلَيْهِ and guides to Him Through guidance (الإرشاد) and divine success (التوفيق). مَنْ يُنِيبُ whoever returns [to Him]. Whoever turns toward Him. Al-Qummi (said): And they are the Imams (الأئمّة) whom He chose and selected.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): أَنْ أَقِيمُوا الدِّينَ ** He (the Imam) said: The Imam (peace be upon him). وَلا تَتَفَرَّقُوا فِيهِ ** Is an allusion (كناية) to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). ما تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ [Is] the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). مَنْ يَشاءُ ** Is an allusion (كناية) to Ali (peace be upon him).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): We are those for whom Allah prescribed His religion. So He said in His Book: He has prescribed for you (شَرَعَ لَكُمْ), O Family of Muhammad, مِنَ الدِّينِ ما وَصَّى بِهِ نُوحاً ** (of the religion which He enjoined upon Noah). And We have been enjoined with what He enjoined upon Noah, وَالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ (and what We have revealed to you), O Muhammad, وَما وَصَّيْنا بِهِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى وَعِيسى ** (and what We enjoined upon Abraham, Moses, and Jesus). We have been given knowledge, and the knowledge of what We know has reached Us, and We have been entrusted with their knowledge. We are the inheritors of the Prophets of Resolve (أولي العزم من الرسل). أَنْ أَقِيمُوا الدِّينَ ** (—to maintain the religion), O Family of Muhammad, وَلا تَتَفَرَّقُوا فِيهِ ** (and do not be divided therein), and be united (كونوا على جماعة). كَبُرَ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (What you call them to is hard on the polytheists)—those who associate partners with the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him)— ما تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ (what you call them to) is the Wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him). Indeed, O Muhammad, Allah يَهْدِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ يُنِيبُ ** (and guides to Him whoever returns [to Him])—whoever responds to you regarding the Wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him).

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq) from al-Sajjad (peace be upon them both), and in al-Kafi, narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq) (peace be upon him): Regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: كَبُرَ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (What you call them to is hard on the polytheists)—[those who associate partners] with the Wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him)— ما تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ ** (what you call them to), O Muhammad, is the Wilayah of Ali (peace be upon him). Thus it is written in the manuscript (مخطوطة).

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Noah to his people [saying]: "Worship Allah, fear Him, and obey me." Then he called them to Allah alone, that they should worship Him and not associate anything with Him. Then He sent the Prophets upon that path until they reached Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his progeny and them all). So he called them to worship Allah and not associate anything with Him. And He said: شَرَعَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الدِّينِ ** (He has prescribed for you the religion) up to His saying مَنْ يُنِيبُ ** (whoever returns [to Him]). So He sent the Prophets to their people with the testimony that there is no god but Allah (لا إله إلّا الله) and the acknowledgment of what came from Allah. Whoever believed sincerely and died upon that, Allah will admit him to Paradise because of it. And that is أَنَّ اللهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ* ** that Allah is not tyrannical to the servants. And that is because Allah would not punish a servant until He had been severe with him regarding murder and the acts of disobedience (المعاصي) for which Allah made the Fire obligatory upon him and upon whoever committed them. When the believers among his people responded to him, He appointed for every Prophet among them شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهاجاً ** a law and a way. And the Shir'ah (الشرعة) and the Minhaj (المنهاج) are a path (سبيل) and a tradition (سنّة).

(١٤) ** وَما تَفَرَّقُوا إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ بَغْياً بَيْنَهُمْ.** (14) They did not split up except after knowledge had come to them, out of envy among themselves.

Al-Qummi said: They did not split up out of ignorance, but they split up when [the knowledge] came to them and they recognized it. Then some of them envied others, and some of them acted wrongfully toward others, when they saw the merits (تفاضيل) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) by the command of Allah. So, they split up into different schools of thought (المذاهب) and adopted [different] opinions (الآراء) and desires (الأهواء). وَلَوْ لا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِنْ رَبِّكَ بالامهال إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ. And were it not for a word that went before from your Lord [granting] a respite until a specified term, judgment would have been made between them.

Al-Qummi said: They said, "Were it not that Allah had decreed that this should be in the first decree (التقدير الأوّل), judgment would have been made between them when they differed, and He would have destroyed them and not granted them respite. But He deferred them إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** (until a specified term), the decreed [term]." وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ أُورِثُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِنْهُ مُرِيبٍ ** And indeed, those who inherited the Book after them are in grave doubt concerning it.

He said: [This is] an allusion (كناية) to those who violated the command of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(١٥) ** فَلِذلِكَ فَادْعُ وَاسْتَقِمْ كَما أُمِرْتَ ** (15) So call [them] to that, and be steadfast as you have been commanded, He said: meaning, for these matters and the religion whose mention preceded, and the loyalty (موالاة) to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), ** فَادْعُ ** (call [them]). And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): meaning, to the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). ** وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَهُمْ ** and do not follow their desires, regarding it. ** وَقُلْ آمَنْتُ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ مِنْ كِتابٍ ** and say, 'I believe in whatever Book Allah has sent down, meaning all the revealed Books. ** وَأُمِرْتُ لِأَعْدِلَ بَيْنَكُمُ اللهُ رَبُّنا وَرَبُّكُمْ ** and I have been commanded to do justice among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. the Creator of all and the Manager of His affair. ** لَنا أَعْمالُنا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمالُكُمْ ** Our deeds belong to us and your deeds belong to you. and everyone will be recompensed according to his deed. ** لا حُجَّةَ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكُمُ ** (There is no argument between us and you). No disputation (لا حجاج), meaning no quarrel (لا خصومة), since the truth has become manifest and there remains no scope for argumentation (للمحاجّة). ** اللهُ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنا ** Allah will bring us together, on the Day of Resurrection. ** وَإِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ ** and toward Him is the destination.' the return of all.

(١٦) وَالَّذِينَ يُحَاجُّونَ فِي اللهِ ** (16) And those who dispute concerning Allah [meaning] in His religion, مِنْ بَعْدِ ما اسْتُجِيبَ لَهُ ** after He has been responded to [meaning] for His religion or for His Messenger. حُجَّتُهُمْ داحِضَةٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ their argument is refuted before their Lord Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: They dispute against Allah after Allah willed to send Messengers to them, and Allah sent the Messengers and the Books to them, but they altered and changed [them]. Then they will dispute on the Day of Resurrection, and their argument against Allah is داحِضَةٌ ** (refuted), meaning, invalid (باطلة), عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ ** (before their Lord, and there is wrath upon them, and there is a severe punishment for them) due to their obstinacy (بمعاندتهم).

(١٧) اللهُ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِّ وَالْمِيزانَ. (Allah is He Who sent down the Book with the truth, and the Balance.) Al-Qummi said: الْمِيزانَ ** (the Balance) refers to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَما يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ السَّاعَةَ قَرِيبٌ ** (What will inform you? Maybe the Hour is near) means its coming (إتيانها). (١٨) يَسْتَعْجِلُ بِهَا الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِها ** (Those who do not believe in it seek to hasten it) mockingly (استهزاء). وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مُشْفِقُونَ مِنْها ** (but those who have faith are fearful of it) meaning they are afraid (خائفون) of it, while also being concerned (اعتناء بها) due to the expectation of reward (لتوقع الثواب). وَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهَا الْحَقُ ** (and know that it is the truth) meaning that which will inevitably occur (الكائن لا محالة). أَلا إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُمارُونَ فِي السَّاعَةِ لَفِي ضَلالٍ بَعِيدٍ. (Look! Indeed, those who dispute concerning the Hour are certainly in a deep error.) Al-Qummi said: It is an allusion (كناية) to the Resurrection (القيامة), for they used to say to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Bring the Hour upon us," فَأْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (So bring us what You promise us, if you are among the truthful). So Allah the Almighty said: أَلا إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُمارُونَ فِي السَّاعَةِ (Look! Indeed, those who dispute concerning the Hour), meaning they contend/quarrel (يخاصمون).

(١٩) اللهُ لَطِيفٌ بِعِبادِهِ (19) Allah is gentle with His servants; ** through varieties of kindness (البرّ). *يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشاءُ He provides for whomever He wishes, It was said: meaning, He provides for him as He wishes, so He singles out each one of His servants with a type of goodness (البرّ) according to what His wisdom necessitated. وَهُوَ الْقَوِيُّ الْعَزِيزُ* and He is the All-strong, the All-mighty. The Unassailable Who is not overcome.

(٢٠) ** مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الْآخِرَةِ ** (20) Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, [Meaning] its reward. He likened it to cultivation (الزرع) because it is a benefit that is obtained through the deeds of this world (الدنيا). For this reason, it was said: "The world (الدنيا) is the cultivation field (مزرعة) of the Hereafter (الآخرة)." ** نَزِدْ لَهُ فِي حَرْثِهِ ** We shall increase him in his harvest. So We give him ten for one, up to seven hundred, and more than that. وَ مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الدُّنْيا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْها And whoever desires the harvest of the world, We shall give him a part of it, something from it, according to what We have apportioned for him. ** وَما لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ نَصِيبٍ ** and he will have no share in the Hereafter. Since deeds are judged by intentions (النيّات), and every person shall only have what he intended (نوى).

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Wealth and sons are the harvest of the world, and righteous deeds are the harvest of the Hereafter. And Allah may combine both of them for some people.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever desires the narration (الحديث) for the benefit of this world (الدنيا) will have no share in the Hereafter. And whoever desires through it the good of the Hereafter (الآخرة), Allah will grant him the good of this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخرة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Whoever has the intention (نيّته) of this world (الدنيا), Allah will scatter his affairs for him and place poverty between his eyes, and He will not grant him anything of this world (الدنيا) except what has been decreed for him. And whoever has the intention (نيّته) of the Hereafter (الآخرة), Allah will gather his affairs for him, place his richness (غناه) in his heart, and the world (الدنيا) will come to him submissively (وهي راغمة).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked regarding The Almighty's saying: ** اللهُ لَطِيفٌ بِعِبادِهِ يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** Allah is gentle with His servants. He provides for whomever He wishes. He said: "It is the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." He was asked regarding ** مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الْآخِرَةِ ** Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter. He said: "It is the recognition (معرفة) of the Commander of the Faithful and the Imams (peace be upon them)." He was asked regarding ** نَزِدْ لَهُ فِي حَرْثِهِ ** We shall increase him in his harvest. He said: "We increase him from it, [so that] he fulfills his share in their state (دولتهم)." [He was asked regarding] وَ مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ الدُّنْيا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْها وَما لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ نَصِيبٍ And whoever desires the harvest of the world, We shall give him a part of it, and he will have no share in the Hereafter. He said: "He has no share in the state of truth (دولة الحقّ) alongside the Imam (الإمام)."

** أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكاءُ شَرَعُوا لَهُمْ مِنَ الدِّينِ ما لَمْ يَأْذَنْ بِهِ اللهُ ** (21) Do they have partners who have prescribed for them a religion that Allah has not permitted? such as polytheism (الشرك), the denial of the Resurrection (إنكار البعث), and working for the world (العمل للدنيا). وَلَوْ لا كَلِمَةُ الْفَصْلِ لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ. Were it not for the word of judgement, it would have been decided between them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "Were it not for what preceded concerning them from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic is His mention, the Qā’im (القائم) would not leave a single one of them remaining." I say: He means the Qā’im (القائم) of every era. وَإِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. Indeed, there is a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.

(٢٢) تَرَى الظَّالِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا ** (22) You will see the wrongdoers fearful of what they have earned fearful of what they committed and did, وَهُوَ واقِعٌ بِهِمْ ** and it is befalling them, meaning: that which they fear. وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فِي رَوْضاتِ الْجَنَّاتِ لَهُمْ ما يَشاؤُنَ (١) and those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be in the meadows of the gardens. They will have whatever they wish عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَضْلُ الْكَبِيرُ. with their Lord. That is the great grace.

** ذلِكَ الَّذِي يُبَشِّرُ اللهُ عِبادَهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ** (23) That is what Allah gives the good news of to His servants who have faith and do righteous deeds.

And it was recited (يُبَشِّرُ) from (أَبْشَرَهُ) (meaning, He gave him good news). قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it) for what I undertake of conveying [the message] (أَجْراً) (a benefit) from you إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى ** (except love of [my] relatives.') that you love my relatives (قَرَابَتِي) and my progeny (عِتْرَتِي) and preserve my honor through them.

This is likewise mentioned in al-Majma' narrated from al-Sajjad, al-Baqir, and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned from the Farewell Pilgrimage (حِجَّةِ الْوَدَاع) and arrived in Medina, the Ansar came to him and said: "O Messenger of Allah, Allah the Almighty has been good to us and honored us through you and your settling among us. Allah has gladdened our friend and suppressed our enemy. Delegations may come to you, and you may not find anything to give them, so the enemy will rejoice over you. We would like you to take a third of our wealth so that when a delegation from Mecca comes to you, you will find something to give them." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not respond to them with anything, waiting for what would come to Him from His Lord. Then Gabriel descended upon Him and said: **قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى ** (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it except love of [my] relatives.') And He did not accept their wealth.

Then the hypocrites (المُنَافِقُونَ) said: "Allah did not reveal this to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He only wants to elevate his cousin's armpit (بِضَبْعِ ابْنِ عَمِّهِ) and impose his Household (أَهْلَ بَيْتِهِ) upon us. Yesterday he said: 'Of whomsoever I am the master (مَوْلَاه), Ali is his master (مَوْلَاه),' and today: قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى. (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it except love of [my] relatives.')"

And in Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from him (al-Sadiq) from his forefathers (peace be upon them): When this Ayah was revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم stood up and said: "O people, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, has enjoined upon you an obligation for me. Will you fulfill it?" He said: None of them answered Him, so He departed. When it was the next day, He stood up and said the same thing. Then He stood among them and said the same thing on the third day, but no one spoke. He then said: "O people, it is not gold, nor silver, nor food, nor drink." They said: "Then tell us what it is." He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed to me: **قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى ** (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it except love of [my] relatives.')" They said: "As for this, yes."

Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, none fulfilled it except seven individuals: Salman, Abu Dharr, Ammar, al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad al-Kindi, Jabir ibn Abd Allah al-Ansari, a freed slave (مَوْلَى) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called al-Bayt, and Zayd ibn Arqam."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to this, with elaboration and explanation. And in al-Jawami', it is narrated: The polytheists (المُشْرِكِينَ) said among themselves, "Do you think that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asks for a reward (أَجْراً) for what He undertakes?" So this Ayah was revealed. Other narrations concerning this Ayah will follow shortly, if Allah wills.

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "By Allah, it is an obligation (فَرِيضَة) from Allah upon the servants for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning His Household (أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "What do the people of Basra say about this Ayah: قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ (Say, 'I do not ask you...') the Ayah?" It was said: "They say that it refers to the relatives (أَقَارِب) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." He said: "They lie. It was revealed specifically concerning us, the People of the House (أَهْلِ الْبَيْت): concerning Ali, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them), the Companions of the Cloak (أَصْحَابِ الْكِسَاء)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas, he said: When this Ayah was revealed: قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ ** (Say, 'I do not ask you...') the Ayah, they said: "O Messenger of Allah, who are these people whose love (مُوَالَاتِهِم) Allah has commanded us [to have]?" He said: "Ali, Fatima, and their children (peace be upon them)."

And narrated from Ali (peace be upon him), he said: "There is an Ayah concerning us, the Āl Ḥā Mīm (آلِ حَم), whose love (مَوَدَّتَنَا) is not preserved except by every believer." Then He recited this Ayah.

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, Allah created the Prophets from various trees, but I and Ali were created from a single tree. I am its root (أَصْلُهَا), Ali is its branch (فَرْعُهَا), Fatima is its pollen (لِقَاحُهَا), al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) are its fruits (ثِمَارُهَا), and our followers (أَشْيَاعُنَا) are its leaves (أَوْرَاقُهَا). Whoever clings to a branch of its branches is saved, and whoever deviates falls. If a servant were to worship Allah between al-Safa and al-Marwah for a thousand years, then another thousand years, then another thousand years, until he becomes like a worn-out waterskin (الشَّنِّ الْبَالِي), yet he did not attain our love (مَحَبَّتَنَا), Allah would cast him down upon his nostrils." Then He recited: **قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ ** (Say, 'I do not ask you...') the Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: "They are the Imams (الأَئِمَّة) (peace be upon them)."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from Ali (peace be upon him), he said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever does not love my progeny (عِتْرَتِي) is one of three: either a hypocrite (مُنَافِق), or born of adultery (لِزِنْيَة), or his mother conceived him in a state of impurity (غَيْرِ طُهْر)." وَمَنْ يَقْتَرِفْ حَسَنَةً نَزِدْ لَهُ فِيها حُسْناً إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ (And whoever commits a good deed, We shall enhance its goodness for him. Indeed, Allah is all-forgiving, all-appreciative.)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That it was revealed concerning us, the People of the House (أَهْلِ الْبَيْت), the Companions of the Cloak (أَصْحَابِ الْكِسَاء) (peace be upon them).

And narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him): He said in a sermon (خُطْبَة): "I am from the Household (أَهْلِ بَيْت) whose love Allah has made obligatory upon every Muslim." Then He recited: قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ ** (Say, 'I do not ask you...') up to His saying: حُسْناً ** (goodness). He said: "So the commitment of a good deed (اقْتِرَافُ الْحَسَنَة) is our love, the love of the People of the House (peace be upon them)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah, He said: "Whoever loves (تَوَالَى) the Successors (الأَوْصِيَاء) from the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and follows their traces, We increase for him the love (وِلَايَة) of the Prophets and the first believers who have passed, until their love reaches Adam (peace be upon him)."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): The commitment (الاِقْتِرَاف) is submission (التَّسْلِيم) to us, being truthful concerning us, and not lying about us.

(1) Meaning: **لَهُمْ ما ** (They shall have whatever) they wish for and desire on the Day of Resurrection.

** أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً فَإِنْ يَشَإِ اللهُ يَخْتِمْ عَلى قَلْبِكَ ** (Or do they say, 'He has fabricated a lie against Allah'? If Allah wishes, He will seal your heart.) by withholding the revelation (بإمساك الوحي).

And it was said that this is a rejection of the possibility of fabrication from someone like him (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), indicating that only one whose heart is sealed and who is ignorant of his Lord would dare to commit such an act. As for one who possesses insight (بصيرة) and knowledge (معرفة), he would not.

And it is as if He said: فَإِنْ يَشَإِ اللهُ ** (to abandon you (خذلانك)), يَخْتِمْ عَلى قَلْبِكَ ** (so that you would dare to fabricate a lie against Him). وَيَمْحُ اللهُ الْباطِلَ ** (the fabricated lie (المفتري)) وَيُحِقُّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِماتِهِ إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ.** (and Allah will obliterate the falsehood and confirm the truth with His words. Indeed, He is aware of what is in the breasts.)

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: "If I wished, I would withhold the revelation from you, and you would not speak concerning the virtue of your Household (أهل بيتك) nor their affection (مودّتهم)." And Allah the Almighty has said: وَيَمْحُ اللهُ الْباطِلَ وَيُحِقُّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِماتِهِ ** He says: He confirms the guardianship (الولاية) for your Household. إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ ** He says: [He is aware of] the enmity (العداوة) toward your Household and the injustice (الظلم) after you, which they cast into their breasts.

Al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that he said: The Ansar came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "We have sheltered and supported [you], so take a portion of our wealth and seek help with it for whatever befalls you." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً ** (meaning, for the prophethood (النبوّة)) إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى ** (meaning, toward his Household (أهل بيته)).

Then he said: "Do you not see that a man may have a friend, yet there is something in the soul of that man against his friend's Household, so his breast is not sound? Thus, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, desired that there should be nothing in the soul of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله against his community (أمته). So Allah obligated upon them the affection (المودّة) toward the relatives (القربى). If they accept it, they accept an obligation (مفروضاً), and if they abandon it, they abandon an obligation."

He said: So they departed from him, and some of them said: "We offered him our wealth, and he said: 'No, fight on behalf of my Household after me.'" And a group said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله did not say this," and they denied it. And they said, as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, recounted: **أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً **

So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَإِنْ يَشَإِ اللهُ يَخْتِمْ عَلى قَلْبِكَ He said: "If you had fabricated a lie, وَيَمْحُ اللهُ الْباطِلَ ** (meaning, He will nullify it (يبطله)) وَيُحِقُّ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِماتِهِ ** (meaning, through the Imams (الأئمّة) and the Qā’im (القائم) from the Household of Muhammad, prayers of Allah be upon them)."

**٢٥ وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَعْفُوا عَنِ السَّيِّئاتِ وَيَعْلَمُ ما تَفْعَلُونَ ** (25) And He is the One who accepts the repentance of His servants, and excuses their misdeeds, and He knows what you do.

And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياء).

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Master of the Martyrs (سيّد الشهداء) (peace be upon him), he said: The Emigrants (المهاجرون) and the Helpers (الأنصار) gathered before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, you have an expense (مؤنة) in your provision and in those who come to you from the delegations (الوفود). These are our wealth and our blood, so judge justly (بارّاً), being rewarded (مأجوراً). Give what You will and withhold what You will, without constraint (من غير حرج)."

He said: Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down the Trustworthy Spirit (الروح الأمين) upon him, and He said: قُلْ ** (Say) O Muhammad لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً إِلَّا الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبى ** (I do not ask you any reward for it except the affection for [My] relatives). Meaning: that you show affection to My relatives after Me.

So they departed. Then the hypocrites (المنافقون) said: "Nothing compelled the Messenger of Allah to reject what we offered him except to urge us toward his relatives after him. It is nothing but something Muhammad fabricated in his gathering." And that statement was considered grave (عظيماً) in their saying.

Then Allah the Almighty sent down this Ayah: **أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَراهُ قُلْ إِنِ افْتَرَيْتُهُ فَلا تَمْلِكُونَ لِي مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِما تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ كَفى بِهِ شَهِيداً بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ وَهُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ ** (Or do they say, 'He has fabricated it'? Say, 'If I have fabricated it, you have no power to help me against Allah. He knows best what you are engrossed in. He is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.)

So the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent for them and asked: "Has anything happened (هل من حدث)?" They replied: "Yes, by Allah, O Messenger of Allah, some of us said a grave statement (كلاماً عظيماً) which we disliked." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited the Ayah to them, and they wept, and their weeping intensified.

Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ (And He is the One who accepts the repentance) [the rest of] the Ayah.

(٢٦) وَيَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَيَزِيدُهُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ (26) And He answers those who have faith and do righteous deeds, and He increases them out of His grace. وَالْكافِرُونَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ. And as for the faithless, there is a severe punishment for them.

In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله arrived in Medina and Islam became firmly established, the Ansar (الأنصار) said among themselves: "Let us go to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and tell Him that matters befall you; these are our properties, rule over them without any constraint (حرج) or prohibition (محظور) upon you." So they came to Him regarding that, and the verse قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ (Say: I do not ask you...) was revealed. He recited it to them and said: "You should love My close kin (قرابتي) after Me." They departed from Him, submitting to His saying. Then the Munafiqun (المنافقون) said: "This is surely something He fabricated in His gathering; He intended to subjugate us to His close kin after Him." Then the verse أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً (Or do they say, 'He has fabricated a lie against Allah'?) was revealed. So, He sent for them and recited it to them, and they wept, and it became severe for them. Then Allah revealed: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ ** (And He is the One who accepts repentance from His servants...)—the verse. So, He sent after them and gave them glad tidings, and said: وَيَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (And He answers those who have faith), and they are those who submitted to His saying.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding the Almighty's saying: وَيَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (And He answers those who have faith)—it refers to the believer who supplicates for his brother in the absence (بظهر الغيب). The angel says to him: "Amen," and the All-mighty, the All-compeller (العزيز الجبّار) says: "And for you is the like of what you asked, and I have granted what you asked due to your love for him."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said regarding: وَيَزِيدُهُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (and He increases them out of His grace)—it is the intercession (الشفاعة) for those for whom the Fire (النار) became incumbent, from among those to whom they showed kindness in the world (الدنيا).

(٢٧) وَلَوْ بَسَطَ اللهُ الرِّزْقَ لِعِبادِهِ لَبَغَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (27) Had Allah extended the provision for His servants, they would have surely rebelled in the earth. So that they would become arrogant and cause corruption out of insolence (بطراً). Al-Qummi said that al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "Had He done so, they would have done so [i.e., rebelled]. But He made some of them dependent upon others, and thereby enslaved them. If He had made all of them wealthy, they would have surely rebelled. وَلكِنْ يُنَزِّلُ بِقَدَرٍ ما يَشاءُ (but He sends down in measure whatever He wishes)." He said: [He sends down] with what He knows will benefit them in their religion (دينهم) and their worldly life (دنياهُم). إِنَّهُ بِعِبادِهِ خَبِيرٌ بَصِيرٌ (Indeed, He is all-aware and all-seeing of His servants.) In the Hadith Qudsi: "Indeed, among My servants are those whom nothing benefits except wealth (الغنى); were I to impoverish him, it would corrupt him. And indeed, among My servants are those whom nothing benefits except poverty (الفقر); were I to enrich him, it would corrupt him. That is because I manage My servants due to My knowledge of their hearts."

(٢٨) وَهُوَ الَّذِي يُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ ** (28) (It is He who sends down the rain) The rain (المطر) which rescues them from drought (الجدب). For this reason, it is specified for the beneficial [rain]. And it was recited يُنَزِّلُ with tashdid (doubling). مِنْ بَعْدِ ما قَنَطُوا ** (after they had despaired) They despaired of it. وَيَنْشُرُ رَحْمَتَهُ ** (and unfolds His mercy) In everything, including the plain (السهل), the mountain (الجبل), the vegetation (النبات), and the animal (الحيوان). وَهُوَ الْوَلِيُ ** (and He is the Guardian) The One Who takes charge of His servants through His benevolence (إحسانه) and the unfolding of His mercy. الْحَمِيدُ ** (the All-laudable.) The One deserving of praise (الحمد).

** وَمِنْ آياتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَثَّ فِيهِما مِنْ دابَّةٍ وَهُوَ عَلى جَمْعِهِمْ إِذا يَشاءُ قَدِيرٌ. (29) And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and whatever He has scattered in them of moving creatures. And He is able to gather them whenever He wishes. ** وَما أَصابَكُمْ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فَبِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ ** (30) Whatever affliction that may befall you is because of what your hands have earned, so it is due to your acts of disobedience (معاصيكم). And it was recited without the fā’ (فاء). وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ ** and He excuses much. of the sins (الذنوب), so He does not punish for them. And the verse is specific to the wrongdoers (المجرمين), for whatever afflicts others is for the increase of reward (الأجر).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "There is no twisting of a vein (التواء عرق), nor striking against a stone (نكبة حجر), nor stumbling of a foot (عثرة قدم), nor scratching by a stick (خدش عود), except due to a sin (بذنب). And what Allah excuses is much more. So, whoever Allah hastens the punishment (عقوبة) for his sin in this world, then Allah is too Glorious (أجلّ), too Generous (أكرم), and too Great (أعظم) to return to punishing him in the Hereafter (الآخرة)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Do you see what afflicted Ali (عليّاً) and his Household (أهل بيته) from these people after him? Was it because of what their hands had earned, while they are the Household of Purity (أهل بيت طهارة), the infallible (معصومون)?" He (peace be upon him) replied: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to repent to Allah and seek His forgiveness a hundred times every day and night, without having committed a sin (من غير ذنب). Indeed, Allah singles out His friends (أولياءه) with afflictions (بالمصائب) so that He may reward them for them, without having committed a sin."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The best verse (آية) in the Book of Allah is this verse. O Ali (يا عليّ), there is no scratching by a stick (خدش عود) nor striking of a foot (نكبة قدم) except due to a sin (بذنب). And whatever Allah excuses in this world, He is too Generous (أكرم) to return to it (the punishment). And whatever He punishes for in this world, He is too Just (أعدل) to repeat it upon His servant."

(٣١) وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (31) You cannot frustrate [Him] on the earth, escaping what He has decreed upon you of calamities وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍ ** and besides Allah you have no guardian who protects you from them, وَلا نَصِيرٍ (nor any helper) who repels them from you. (٣٢) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ الْجَوارِ ** (32) Among His signs are the ships the sailing vessels فِي الْبَحْرِ كَالْأَعْلامِ ** on the sea, like mountains. like mountains. (٣٣) إِنْ يَشَأْ يُسْكِنِ الرِّيحَ فَيَظْلَلْنَ رَواكِدَ عَلى ظَهْرِهِ ** (33) If He wishes, He stills the wind, and they remain motionless on its surface. so they remain stationary upon the surface of the sea إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ ** There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [person]. for everyone who entrusts his resolve and restrains his soul to contemplate the signs of Allah and reflect upon His bounties (آلائه), or for every believer perfect in faith, for faith is two halves: half patience (صبر) and half gratitude (شكر), as mentioned in the Hadith.

(٣٤) أَوْ يُوبِقْهُنَ ** (34) Or He destroys them or He destroys them, meaning, their people, by sending the violent, drowning winds, بِما كَسَبُوا وَيَعْفُ عَنْ كَثِيرٍ ** by saving them. (35) ** وَيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ يُجادِلُونَ فِي آياتِنا ** (35) and that those who dispute concerning Our signs may know It is said that it is conjoined (عطف) to an implied reason (علّة مقدرة), such as: so that He may take vengeance upon them and know. And it was read with the raf' (الرفع) (nominative case) based on beginning a new sentence (الاستيناف). ما لَهُمْ مِنْ مَحِيصٍ ** a turning away (محيد) from the punishment. (36) ** فَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَمَتاعُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (36) Whatever you have been given is the provision of the life of the world you enjoy it for the duration of your lives. وَما عِنْدَ اللهِ ** of the reward of the Hereafter خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقى ** due to the purity of its benefit and its permanence لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ. but what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who have faith and rely on their Lord.**

(37) وَالَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبائِرَ الْإِثْمِ وَالْفَواحِشَ ** (37) and those who avoid major sins and indecencies, And it was recited (كبير الإثم). The explanation of the major sins (الكبائر) has already preceded in Surat an-Nisa. وَإِذا ما غَضِبُوا هُمْ يَغْفِرُونَ. and when they are angry, they forgive. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: "Whoever suppresses rage (غيظاً) while being able to execute it (إمضائه), Allah will fill his heart with security (أمْناً) and faith (إيماناً) on the Day of Resurrection." He said: "And whoever controls his soul when he desires (رغب), and when he fears (رهب), and when he is angry (غضب), Allah will forbid his body from the Fire." And concerning this meaning, there are many narrations (أخبار كثيرة) in al-Kafi and other sources.

(38) وَالَّذِينَ اسْتَجابُوا لِرَبِّهِمْ (and those who respond to their Lord) they accepted what they were commanded with. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said regarding the establishment of the Imamate (إقامة الامام): وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَمْرُهُمْ شُورى بَيْنَهُمْ (and maintain the prayer, and whose affairs are [settled through] consultation among themselves) consultation (تشاور) among them, and they do not act independently based on a single opinion until they consult and agree upon it, and that is due to the extreme vigilance (تيقّظهم) they possess in matters. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: They consult the Imam (peace be upon him) regarding what they need concerning the matter of their religion, just as Allah said: وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ. (Had they referred it to the Apostle and to those vested with authority among them [i.e., the Imams].) And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: There is no man who consults anyone except that he is guided to rectitude (الرّشد). وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ (and spend out of what We have provided them) in the path of goodness.

(٣٩)** وَالَّذِينَ إِذا أَصابَهُمُ الْبَغْيُ هُمْ يَنْتَصِرُونَ ** (39) And those who, when tyranny befalls them, defend themselves.

According to what Allah has made permissible for them, out of dislike for humiliation (التذلّل). And this is His description of them with courage (الشجاعة) after His description of them with the rest of the fundamental virtues (أمّهات الفضائل). And this does not contradict His description of them with forgiveness (الغفران), for forgiveness (الغفران) implies the inability (عجز) of the one forgiven, while seeking victory (الانتصار) indicates resistance (مقاومة) against the adversary (الخصم). Forbearance (الحلم) towards the incapable (العاجز) is praiseworthy (محمود), but towards the dominant (المتغلب) is blameworthy (مذموم), because it is an encouragement (إجراء) and incitement (إغراء) to tyranny (البغي).

** وَجَزاءُ سَيِّئَةٍ سَيِّئَةٌ مِثْلُها ** (40) The recompense of a bad deed is a bad deed similar to it. He named the second (سيّئة) a bad deed (سيّئة) due to pairing (للازدواج) or because it grieves (تسوء) the one upon whom it descends. This is a prohibition against transgression (التعدي) in seeking victory (الانتصار).

فَمَنْ عَفا وَأَصْلَحَ ** But whoever pardons and sets things right, between himself and his enemy, فَأَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللهِ ** (his reward rests with Allah). This is an ambiguous promise (عِدَة مبهمة) that indicates the greatness of what is promised (الموعود).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He said: "When the Day of Resurrection comes, a caller will cry out: 'Whoever's reward rests with Allah (أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللهِ), let him enter Paradise.' It will be asked: 'Who is this whose reward rests with Allah?' It will be said: 'Those who pardon people (العافون عن الناس). They shall enter Paradise without reckoning (بغير حساب)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Hold fast to pardon (العفو), for pardon does not increase a servant except in honor (عزّاً). So pardon one another, and Allah will honor you."

إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ Indeed, He does not love the wrongdoers. (He does not love) those who initiate the bad deed (المبتدءين بالسّيّئة) and those who transgress in seeking revenge (المتجاوزين في الانتقام).

(٤١)وَلَمَنِ انْتَصَرَ بَعْدَ ظُلْمِهِ (41) And whoever vindicates himself after being wronged, after he was wronged فَأُولئِكَ ما عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ then there is no way open against them. by way of reproach (المعاتبة) or punishment (المعاقبة). In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): And the right (حقّ) of the one who wronged you is that you forgive him, but if you know that forgiveness is harmful, you vindicate yourself. The Almighty Allah said: وَلَمَنِ انْتَصَرَ بَعْدَ ظُلْمِهِ فَأُولئِكَ مَا عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ. (And whoever vindicates himself after being wronged, then there is no way open against them.) And narrated from al-Sadiq, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Three types of people, if you do not wrong them, they will wrong you: the base people (السفلة), the wife (الزّوجة), and the slave (المملوك).

(٤٢) إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَظْلِمُونَ النَّاسَ ** (42) The way [of blame] is only against those who wrong the people They initiate harm against them and seek what they do not deserve out of arrogance (تجبّراً) against them. وَيَبْغُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** and commit aggression on the earth unjustly. For such there is a painful punishment. due to their injustice (ظلمهم) and aggression (بغيهم).

(٤٣) وَلَمَنْ صَبَرَ ** (But whoever is patient) upon harm (الأذى) ** وَغَفَرَ ** (and forgives) and does not seek vengeance ** إِنَّ ذلِكَ لَمِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ ** (that is indeed one of the matters requiring courage.) meaning ** إِنَّ ذلِكَ ** (that) from him ** لَمِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ. (is indeed one of the matters requiring courage.)

(٤٤) وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ ** (Whomever Allah leads astray, there is no guardian for him besides Him.) meaning, no helper (نَاصِر) who takes him as a guardian (يَتَوَلَّاهُ) after Allah has forsaken him (خِذْلَان الله إِيَّاهُ). وَتَرَى الظَّالِمِينَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذابَ (And you will see the wrongdoers, when they sight the punishment) when they see it ** يَقُولُونَ هَلْ إِلى مَرَدٍّ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ ** (saying, 'Is there any way back?') meaning, is there any return (رَجْعَة) to the world (الدُّنْيَا).

(٤٥) وَتَراهُمْ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْها ** (45) And you will see them being presented to it meaning, to the Fire (النار), and the word 'torment' (العذاب) indicates this. خاشِعِينَ مِنَ الذُّلِ humbled by abasement meaning, submissive (متذلّلين), shrinking back (متقاصرين) due to the abasement (الذل) that afflicts them. يَنْظُرُونَ مِنْ طَرْفٍ خَفِيٍ ** looking with a furtive glance meaning, their gaze towards the Fire begins with a weak movement of their eyelids, like one who is held captive (كالمصبور) looking at the sword. وَقالَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ الْخاسِرِينَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ ** And those who have faith will say, 'Indeed the losers are those who have ruined themselves and their families by exposing them to the eternal torment (العذاب المخلّد). يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ أَلا إِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ فِي عَذابٍ مُقِيمٍ. on the Day of Resurrection. Look! The wrongdoers shall be in a lasting torment.'**

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: وَلَمَنِ انْتَصَرَ بَعْدَ ظُلْمِهِ ** And whoever vindicates himself after being wronged He means al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) and his companions. When he rises, he will vindicate himself against Banu Umayyah, the deniers (المكذبين), and the النصاب (those who harbor enmity). He and his companions will do this, and this is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَظْلِمُونَ النَّاسَ [up to] the end of the Ayah. And you will see those who wronged the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) regarding their right, when they see the torment (العذاب). And Ali (عليّ) is the torment (العذاب) in this context. يَقُولُونَ هَلْ إِلى مَرَدٍّ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ ** They will say, 'Is there any way back?' so that we may be loyal to Ali (peace be upon him). وَتَراهُمْ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْها خاشِعِينَ مِنَ الذُّلِ ** And you will see them being presented to it, humbled by abasement due to Ali (peace be upon him). يَنْظُرُونَ الى عليّ عليه السلام مِنْ طَرْفٍ خَفِيٍّ They will look at Ali (peace be upon him) with a furtive glance. وَقالَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا And those who have faith will say meaning, the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) and their followers (شيعتهم). أَلا إِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ ** Look! The wrongdoers those who wronged the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family) regarding their right, فِي عَذابٍ مُقِيمٍ shall be in a lasting torment. He said: By Allah, He means the النصاب (those who harbor enmity) who established enmity towards the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and his progeny (ذرّيته), and the deniers (المكذبين).

(٤٦) وَما كانَ لَهُمْ مِنْ أَوْلِياءَ يَنْصُرُونَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ ** (46) And they will have no allies to help them besides Allah. And whomever Allah leads astray, there is no way for him. to guidance (الهدى) and salvation (النجاة). (٤٧) اسْتَجِيبُوا لِرَبِّكُمْ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لا مَرَدَّ لَهُ مِنَ اللهِ ما لَكُمْ ** from Allah مِنْ مَلْجَإٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ نَكِيرٍ ** (47) Answer your Lord before there comes a day from Allah which cannot be averted. On that day you will have no refuge, nor will you have any possibility of denial. denial (إنكار) of what you committed, because it is affirmed in the scrolls of your deeds, and your limbs (جوارحكم) bear witness to it.

(٤٨) فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا فَما أَرْسَلْناكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظاً ** (48) But if they turn away, We have not sent you to be a guardian over them. meaning: an overseer (رقيباً). (٤٩) إِنْ عَلَيْكَ إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ ** (49) Your duty is only to communicate. And you have already communicated (وقد بلغت). وَإِنَّا إِذا أَذَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةً فَرِحَ بِها وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَإِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ كَفُورٌ ** And when We let man taste a mercy from Us, he rejoices in it; but if an ill befalls them because of what their hands have sent ahead, behold, man is ungrateful. meaning: profoundly ungrateful (بليغ الكفران). He completely forgets the blessing (النعمة) and remembers the affliction (البليّة) and magnifies it, without reflecting upon its cause. And He only introduced the first (statement) with (إذا) (when) and the second with (إنّ) (if) because the tasting of the blessing (النعمة) is certain (محقّقة), contrary to the befalling of the affliction (البليّة). And He only established the cause of the recompense in its place in the second (statement) and placed the explicit noun (الظاهر) in the place of the implicit pronoun (المضمر) to indicate that this species (of man) is marked by ingratitude for the blessing (كفران النعمة).

(٥٠) لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (50) To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. So, it is for Him to divide the bounty (النعمة) and the affliction (البليّة) however He wills. يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ يَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ إِناثاً وَيَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ الذُّكُورَ He creates whatever He wishes. He grants females to whomever He wishes, and grants males to whomever He wishes, أَوْ يُزَوِّجُهُمْ ذُكْراناً وَإِناثاً وَيَجْعَلُ مَنْ يَشاءُ عَقِيماً إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ.** or He couples them, males and females, and He makes whomever He wishes barren. Indeed, He is all-knowing, all-powerful.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): يَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ إِناثاً ** meaning, there is no male with them, and وَيَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ الذُّكُورَ meaning, there is no female with them. أَوْ يُزَوِّجُهُمْ ذُكْراناً وَإِناثاً ** meaning, He grants males (ذكراناً) and females (إناثاً) together to whomever He wills, gathering for him the sons (البنين) and the daughters (البنات), meaning, He grants them all to one person.

(٥١) وَما كانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُكَلِّمَهُ اللهُ إِلَّا وَحْياً ** (51) It is not for any human that Allah should speak to him except by way of revelation, By seeing an angel and hearing from him, or by it falling into his heart without seeing anyone. And the origin (أصل) of al-wahy (الوحي) is the hidden speech (الكلام الخفيّ) which is perceived quickly. أَوْ مِنْ وَراءِ حِجابٍ ** or from behind a curtain, By hearing a sound without seeing (the source). أَوْ يُرْسِلَ رَسُولاً فَيُوحِيَ بِإِذْنِهِ ما يَشاءُ or He sends a messenger who reveals by His leave whatever He wishes. So he hears from the messenger (الرسول).

Al-Qummi said: Revelation (وحي) by direct address (مشافهة) and revelation by inspiration (إلهام), which is that which falls into the heart, أَوْ مِنْ وَراءِ حِجابٍ (or from behind a curtain), just as Allah spoke to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and just as Allah spoke to Moses from the fire. أَوْ يُرْسِلَ رَسُولاً فَيُوحِيَ بِإِذْنِهِ ما يَشاءُ (or He sends a messenger who reveals by His leave whatever He wishes). He said: Revelation by direct address (وحي مشافهة), meaning to the people. إِنَّهُ عَلِيٌ ** Indeed He is all-exalted, Above the attributes of the creatures, حَكِيمٌ (all-wise), He does what His wisdom (حكمته) necessitates.

(٥٢) وَكَذلِكَ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ رُوحاً مِنْ أَمْرِنا ** (52) Thus did We send to you a spirit of Our command. Meaning, We sent it to you through revelation (بالوحي). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "A creation among the creations of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, greater than Gabriel (جبرئيل) and Michael (ميكائيل), was with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, informing him and guiding him, and He is with the Imams (peace be upon them) after him." And in a narration: "Since Allah sent down that Spirit (الروح) upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, it has not ascended to the heaven, and indeed, it is among us." ما كُنْتَ تَدْرِي مَا الْكِتابُ وَلَا الْإِيمانُ ** (You did not know what the Book was, nor what faith was.) Meaning, before the revelation. وَلكِنْ جَعَلْناهُ نُوراً نَهْدِي بِهِ مَنْ نَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِنا. (But We made it a light by which We guide whomever We wish of Our servants.) In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about knowledge (العلم): "Is it something that the scholar learns from the mouths of men, or is it in a book with you that you read and learn from?" He said: "The matter is greater and more incumbent than that. Have you not heard the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَكَذلِكَ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ رُوحاً مِنْ أَمْرِنا ما كُنْتَ تَدْرِي مَا الْكِتابُ وَلَا الْإِيمانُ ** (Thus did We send to you a spirit of Our command. You did not know what the Book was, nor what faith was.)" Then he said: "What do your companions say about this verse? Do they read that He was in a state where He did not know مَا الْكِتابُ وَلَا الْإِيمانُ (what the Book was, nor what faith was)?" So I said: "I do not know, may I be made your ransom, what they say." He said: "Yes, He was in a state where He did not know مَا الْكِتابُ وَلَا الْإِيمانُ ** (what the Book was, nor what faith was), until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent the Spirit (الروح) mentioned in the Book. So when He revealed it to Him, He knew by it knowledge (العلم) and understanding (الفهم). And it is the Spirit (الروح) that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, gives to whomever He wills. When He gives it to a servant, He teaches him understanding (الفهم)." And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: وَلكِنْ جَعَلْناهُ نُوراً ** (But We made it a light). He said: "He means Ali (peace be upon him), and Ali is the Light (النّور) by which He guided whomever He guided among His creation." وَإِنَّكَ لَتَهْدِي إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** (And indeed, you guide to a straight path.) He said: "He means that you command the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) and call to it, and Ali (peace be upon him) is the Straight Path (الصراط المُستقيم)."

(٥٣) صِراطِ اللهِ الَّذِي لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ.** (53) The path of Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth.

He said, meaning ‘Ali (peace be upon him), that He (Allah) made him His treasurer over everything that is in the heavens and what is in the earth, and entrusted him with it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: وَإِنَّكَ لَتَهْدِي إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** (52) and indeed you guide to a straight path— He says: You call to أَلا إِلَى اللهِ تَصِيرُ الْأُمُورُ ** (53) Behold! All matters revert to Allah. by the removal of intermediaries (الوسائط) and attachments (التعلّقات). And in it is a promise and a threat for the obedient and the criminals.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: A Mushaf fell into the sea, and they found it, and everything in it had vanished except for this verse: أَلا إِلَى اللهِ تَصِيرُ الْأُمُورُ. (53) Behold! All matters revert to Allah.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Ha Mim 'Ayn Sin Qaf (Surah Ash-Shura) will be resurrected by Allah on the Day of Resurrection with his face like snow or like the sun, until he stands before Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. He will say: "My servant, you persisted in reciting Ha Mim 'Ayn Sin Qaf, yet you did not know what its reward was. Had you known what it is and what its reward is, you would never have tired of reciting it. But I shall reward you your recompense. Enter him into Paradise." And he will have therein a palace of red ruby, whose doors, balconies (شرفها), and steps (درجها) are made of it. Its exterior will be visible from its interior, and its interior from its exterior. And he will have therein two Huris from the Hūr al-'Īn, a thousand maidservants (جارية), and a thousand youths (غلام) from the immortal youths (الغلمان المخلّدين) whom Allah the Almighty described.
